question_text: Read the source extract shown below from a sixteenth-century European text. What was the writer's chief argument about the new technology?

options.A: It would democratise ideas and weaken authority
options.B: It would only entertain wealthy patrons
options.C: It would replace handwriting in noble households
options.D: It would not reach beyond city walls

answer_key.explanation: The author claims the press could spread radical ideas widely, undermining the established authority of the Church.

visual.caption: Sixteenth-century commentary on print.
visual.altText: Numbered three-line extract from a sixteenth-century source describing the social impact of a new technology.

spec.title: Voices of the print revolution
spec.source: Adapted from a sixteenth-century European text
spec.context: A commentator writes about a recently invented technology and its likely social effect.

lines.text: The mechanical art now permits a single book to be copied many times in a short interval.
Even the unlettered townspeople may soon read what was once reserved for the cloister.
Those who guarded the older learning may find their pulpits less heeded than before.
